description: Adversaries may send phishing messages to gain access to victim systems.
learning_insight: Phishing remains the most reliable initial access vector because it exploits human psychology rather than technical flaws.

description: Adversaries may use brute force techniques to attempt access to accounts when passwords are unknown.
learning_insight: Brute force and volumetric attacks rely on high-frequency automated requests to overwhelm authentication or system resources.

description: Adversaries may abuse command and script interpreters to execute commands, scripts, or binaries.
learning_insight: Fileless malware heavily relies on built-in OS interpreters like PowerShell or bash to evade signature-based antivirus.

description: Adversaries may delete or modify artifacts generated within systems to remove evidence of their presence or hinder defenses.
learning_insight: Advanced attackers often delete Windows Event Logs or alter bash_history immediately upon gaining root access.
